compiled_state.title: Write E2E acceptance test for duplicates and supersedes relations full lifecycle
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Context
This is a single end-to-end test covering: CLI invocation -> event log write -> reducer round-trip -> dep graph output correctness.

## What to do
Create `tests/acceptance/test-duplicates-supersedes-e2e.sh` (or add to existing acceptance test suite).

## Test fixture and assertions
1. Create tickets A, B, C in an isolated scratch tracker directory
2. Run `ticket link A B duplicates` — assert exit 0
3. Run `ticket link C A supersedes` — assert exit 0
4. Run `ticket deps A` — assert:
   - `deps` array contains entry with `target_id: B, relation: "duplicates"`
   - `blockers` array does NOT contain B
   - `ready_to_work: true` (non-blocking relation)
5. Run `ticket deps C` — assert:
   - `deps` array contains entry with `target_id: A, relation: "supersedes"`
   - `blockers` array does NOT contain A
   - `ready_to_work: true`
6. Idempotency: run `ticket link A B duplicates` again — assert exit 0, only ONE LINK event exists for this pair
7. Unlink: run `ticket unlink A B` — assert exit 0
8. Run `ticket deps A` again — assert: B is NOT in deps (cancelled by UNLINK)
9. `ticket show --format=llm A` — assert `dp` array contains `{"r": "duplicates", "tid": "B"}` before unlink step (re-create and re-check)

## Files to create
- `tests/acceptance/test-duplicates-supersedes-e2e.sh` — new acceptance test

## Acceptance criteria
- Test runs in isolated fixture (own temp tracker dir, not real .tickets-tracker)
- All 9 assertions pass after c0a9-91dd and 694b-f1a2 changes are in place
- Test is registered in .test-index
- Script is executable

## Testing mode
RED — writing acceptance test against behavior implemented by c0a9-91dd and 694b-f1a2
